reasoning: The merger was blocked by President Biden, with a CFIUS requirement to abandon the deal extended to June 18, 2025. However, the subsequent election of Donald Trump has revived the deal. While Trump initially said in February 2025 that Nippon should only invest in U.S. Steel, on April 7/8, he ordered a new 45-day CFIUS review of the full merger. U.S. Steel explicitly remained committed to the full $55/share acquisition, and Nippon reportedly floated increasing its investment pledge up to $7 billion to secure approval. U.S. Steel stock surged 16% upon news of the new review, indicating the market views it as a strong positive signal that Trump intends to use the review to declare a "better deal" and approve the transaction. Furthermore, the DOJ delayed lawsuit oral arguments to mid-May to accommodate this. Given that the parties are actively negotiating and creating procedural runways to save the deal, it is highly unlikely they will terminate the merger agreement and file an 8-K by July 1; instead, they will either receive approval or continue extending the deadlines as negotiations progress.
